# Morteza Aghajanzadeh — Academic Website

> PhD Candidate in Finance at Stockholm School of Economics.
> Research: venture capital networks, entrepreneurship, innovation, carbon policy.

## About

Morteza Aghajanzadeh is a PhD candidate in the Department of Finance at the
Stockholm School of Economics. His research examines how venture capital networks
shape startup performance and market entry, and how carbon policies affect capital
reallocation. He combines theoretical modeling with empirical analysis.

Email: morteza.aghajanzadeh@hhs.se
Location: Stockholm, Sweden
Institution: Stockholm School of Economics, Department of Finance

## Research Areas

- Venture Capital, Entrepreneurship, and Innovation
- Network Structure and Market Competition
- Strategic Investment and Entry Dynamics
- Carbon Policy and Capital Reallocation

## Working Papers

- [The Two Faces of VC Networks: Performance Premiums and Entry Deterrence](https://mortezaaghajanzadeh.com/project/jmp/)
  This paper develops a dynamic model of venture capital networks to study how
  financial intermediation shapes startup performance and market entry. It highlights
  three key mechanisms: assortative matching, value-added support, and entry deterrence
  through coordinated behavior — showing how networks can both improve efficiency and
  restrict competition.

- [Reallocation Cost of Carbon Policies](https://mortezaaghajanzadeh.com/project/reallocation-cost-of-carbon-policies/)
  This paper examines the effects of carbon policies on capital reallocation and
  emission reductions in the economy, using a model that captures interactions between
  green and brown capital, productivity, and emissions. Estimated using data from the
  Swedish manufacturing sector (1990–2015). Key finding: emission tax reduces emissions
  by 54%; achieving a 75% reduction by 2040 requires a 270% tax rate increase.
